(born September 17, 1983) is an American [professional boxer](/wiki/Professional_boxer "Professional boxer") who held the [WBA (Unified)](/wiki/World_Boxing_Association "World Boxing Association"), [IBF](/wiki/International_Boxing_Federation "International Boxing Federation") and [WBO](/wiki/World_Boxing_Organization "World Boxing Organization") [lightweight](/wiki/Lightweight "Lightweight") championships from 2007 to 2008 and the [IBO](/wiki/International_Boxing_Organization "International Boxing Organization") lightweight championship from 2008 to 2009.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

American boxer (born 1983)

This article is about the American boxer. For other uses, see [Juan Díaz (disambiguation)](/wiki/Juan_D%C3%ADaz_(disambiguation) "Juan Díaz (disambiguation)").

For the Chilean boxer, see [Juan Díaz (Chilean boxer)](/wiki/Juan_D%C3%ADaz_(Chilean_boxer) "Juan Díaz (Chilean boxer)").

Juan Díaz

| Personal information | |

| Height | 5 ft 6 in (168 cm) |

**Juan Díaz** (born September 17, 1983) is an American [professional boxer](/wiki/Professional_boxer "Professional boxer") who held the [WBA (Unified)](/wiki/World_Boxing_Association "World Boxing Association"), [IBF](/wiki/International_Boxing_Federation "International Boxing Federation") and [WBO](/wiki/World_Boxing_Organization "World Boxing Organization") [lightweight](/wiki/Lightweight "Lightweight") championships from 2007 to 2008 and the [IBO](/wiki/International_Boxing_Organization "International Boxing Organization") lightweight championship from 2008 to 2009.

## Amateur career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Juan_D%C3%ADaz_(boxer)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Amateur career")]

Diaz was 105–5 as an amateur, winning 13 National Gold Medals, two National Silver Medals and four World Championships.[[1]](#cite_note-boxingscene.com-1) He thought he had qualified for the 2000 Mexican Olympic team but was informed he was too young to compete in [Sydney](/wiki/Sydney "Sydney"). He did not want to wait four more years and turned pro.

## Professional career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Juan_D%C3%ADaz_(boxer)&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Professional career")]

### Lightweight

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Juan_D%C3%ADaz_(boxer)&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Lightweight")]

Díaz made his professional boxing debut at age 16 on June 23, 2000 with a first-round [TKO](/wiki/Knockout "Knockout") victory over Rafael Ortiz.[[2]](#cite_note-boxrec-2) He won his first five fights by knockout.

On November 22, 2003, Díaz won the WBO youth lightweight title with a sixth-round TKO of journeyman Joel Pérez.[[2]](#cite_note-boxrec-2) He accumulated a record of 24–0, which included wins over title challengers Joel Perez and [Francisco Lorenzo](/wiki/Francisco_Lorenzo "Francisco Lorenzo"), before challenging for his first world title.

On July 17, 2004, Díaz defeated Mongolian [Lakva Sim](/wiki/Lakva_Sim "Lakva Sim") for the WBA lightweight title by unanimous decision.[[2]](#cite_note-boxrec-2) Díaz defended the title seven times.[[2]](#cite_note-boxrec-2) On December 4, 2004, he defeated former two-time WBA lightweight champion [Julien Lorcy](/wiki/Julien_Lorcy "Julien Lorcy") by unanimous decision. In his second defense, he defeated [Billy Irwin](/wiki/William_Irwin_(boxer) "William Irwin (boxer)") by 9th round tko. On April 8, 2006, he defended his title against undefeated [José Miguel Cotto](/wiki/Jos%C3%A9_Miguel_Cotto "José Miguel Cotto") (brother of [Miguel Cotto](/wiki/Miguel_Cotto "Miguel Cotto")) with a unanimous decision victory.[[2]](#cite_note-boxrec-2) In his next fight, he defeated Randy Suico by 9th round tko. On November 14, 2006, Diaz successfully defended his title for the fifth time against Fernando Angulo of Ecuador by unanimous decision.

On April 28, 2007, Diaz was upgraded to super champion by the [WBA](/wiki/World_Boxing_Association "World Boxing Association"), successfully defending his title against WBO champion [Acelino Freitas](/wiki/Acelino_Freitas "Acelino Freitas") by TKO after Freitas forfeited the fight before beginning the 9th round, unifying the [WBO](/wiki/World_Boxing_Organization "World Boxing Organization") lightweight championship with the WBA (Super) lightweight title. The fight took place in [Mashantucket](/wiki/Mashantucket "Mashantucket"), USA.

On October 13, 2007, Díaz unified a third title by defeating [IBF](/wiki/IBF "IBF") Lightweight champion [Julio Díaz](/wiki/Julio_D%C3%ADaz_(boxer) "Julio Díaz (boxer)") in [Chicago](/wiki/Chicago "Chicago") [Illinois](/wiki/Illinois "Illinois").

#### Diaz vs. Campbell

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Juan_D%C3%ADaz_(boxer)&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Diaz vs. Campbell")]

[Nate Campbell](/wiki/Nate_Campbell "Nate Campbell") defeated Diaz by split decision to become IBF, WBO, WBA Champion, on March 8, 2008 in Cancún, Mexico. This ended Diaz's undefeated streak of thirty-three wins.

Diaz's training is supervised by fitness guru Brian Caldwell of Houston Texas.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

#### Diaz vs. Katsidis

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Juan_D%C3%ADaz_(boxer)&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Diaz vs. Katsidis")]

Díaz (34–1, 17 KO's) won a 12-round [split decision](/wiki/Split_decision "Split decision") over Australia's previous WBO lightweight champion [Michael Katsidis](/wiki/Michael_Katsidis "Michael Katsidis"), (23–2, 20 KO's) on September 6, 2008. Diaz became the new [IBO](/wiki/International_Boxing_Organization "International Boxing Organization") lightweight champion in the "No Retreat, No Surrender" main event fight at the [Toyota Center](/wiki/Toyota_Center_(Houston) "Toyota Center (Houston)") in [Houston, Texas](/wiki/Houston,_Texas "Houston, Texas"). [ringside](/wiki/Ringside_(boxing) "Ringside (boxing)") judge Gale Van Hoy scored the fight 116–112 while Levi Martinez scored it 115–113, all for Diaz, but Judge Glen Hamada had it 115–113 for Katsidis.[[4]](#cite_note-4)[[5]](#cite_note-5)

#### Diaz vs. Marquez

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Juan_D%C3%ADaz_(boxer)&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Diaz vs. Marquez")]

On February 28, 2009, [Juan Manuel Márquez](/wiki/Juan_Manuel_M%C3%A1rquez "Juan Manuel Márquez") defeated Diaz in the 9th round of a 12 round bout for the unified world lightweight title. In the opening rounds, Diaz appeared to be winning the fight, pinning Marquez on the ropes and occasionally staggering him with big left hands. In the eighth round, Diaz was cut over his eye by a Marquez punch, much like he was in the loss to Nate Campbell. After starting strong in the ninth round, Marquez's precision punching proved to be too much and Diaz was knocked down twice, bringing an end to the fight. This fight was named "Fight of the Year" for 2009 by [Ring magazine](/wiki/Ring_magazine "Ring magazine") and ESPN.com.[[6]](#cite_note-6) A rematch was scheduled for July the 31st 2010. The fight was held live on Pay Per View and in U.S. Theaters[[7]](#cite_note-7) around the country.

### Brief foray at light welterweight

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Juan_D%C3%ADaz_(boxer)&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Brief foray at light welterweight")]

#### Diaz vs. Malignaggi I & II

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Juan_D%C3%ADaz_(boxer)&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Diaz vs. Malignaggi I & II")]

On August 22, 2009, Diaz moved up in weight to challenge [Paul Malignaggi](/wiki/Paul_Malignaggi "Paul Malignaggi") for the vacant WBO NABO light welterweight title at a catch-weight of 138½ pounds in his hometown of [Houston, Texas](/wiki/Houston,_Texas "Houston, Texas"). Although the fight was competitive, Malignaggi appeared stronger in the latter rounds. Diaz was ultimately awarded a unanimous decision, however, the scores were controversial with one judge, Gale Von Hoy, scoring the fight 118–110 in Diaz's favor; another, Raul Caiz Sr., had it 115-113. Harold Lederman scored the fight 7 rounds to 5 for Malignaggi. On December 12, he had a rematch vs Paulie Malignaggi and lost a unanimous decision, all 3 judges scored it 116–111.[[8]](#cite_note-8)

### Return to lightweight

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Juan_D%C3%ADaz_(boxer)&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Return to lightweight")]

#### Diaz vs. Marquez II

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Juan_D%C3%ADaz_(boxer)&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Diaz vs. Marquez II")]

Main article: [Juan Manuel Márquez vs. Juan Díaz II](/wiki/Juan_Manuel_M%C3%A1rquez_vs._Juan_D%C3%ADaz_II "Juan Manuel Márquez vs. Juan Díaz II")

Diaz fought Juan Manuel Márquez for the second time on July 31, 2010. Marquez won the fight via Unanimous Decision, 117–111, 118–110, 116–112

### Comeback

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Juan_D%C3%ADaz_(boxer)&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Comeback")]

#### Diaz vs. Pipino Cuevas Jr.

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Juan_D%C3%ADaz_(boxer)&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Diaz vs. Pipino Cuevas Jr.")]

Declaring that he had "the fire back", the former unified lightweight titleholder Juan Díaz ended a 2½-year retirement on April 13 by fighting Gerardo "Pipino" Cuevas Jr. He was trained by Tim Knight of Louisville, Kentucky[[9]](#cite_note-9) He won that bout, and went on fighting until 2016.

## Personal life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Juan_D%C3%ADaz_(boxer)&action=edit&section=13 "Edit section: Personal life")]

Díaz maintained a full-time academic schedule while boxing,[[1]](#cite_note-boxingscene.com-1) graduating high school from [Contemporary Learning Center](/wiki/Contemporary_Learning_Center "Contemporary Learning Center") in Houston in 2001 [[1]](https://web.archive.org/web/20070110203544/http://www.houstonisd.org/HISD/portal/article/front/0,2731,20856_67359489_94477637,00.html) and obtaining a bachelor's degree in [political science](/wiki/Political_science "Political science") from the [University of Houston–Downtown](/wiki/University_of_Houston%E2%80%93Downtown "University of Houston–Downtown"). He is a community activist who has been deputized as a Volunteer Voter Registrar for [Harris County, Texas](/wiki/Harris_County,_Texas "Harris County, Texas"), and has also worked with the League of Women Voters of the Houston Area to promote full participation in civic life.[[10]](#cite_note-10) Diaz's promotional company, Baby Bull LLC, partners with [ESPN Deportes](/wiki/ESPN_Deportes "ESPN Deportes") to produce the radio show *The Baby Bull Show*, which has featured guest appearances by fellow boxers. He also has a trucking company, JD Express Inc, with his brother Jose as partner.[[1]](#cite_note-boxingscene.com-1)

## Professional boxing record

## Titles in boxing

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Juan_D%C3%ADaz_(boxer)&action=edit&section=15 "Edit section: Titles in boxing")]

### Major world titles

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Juan_D%C3%ADaz_(boxer)&action=edit&section=16 "Edit section: Major world titles")]

* [WBA (Unified and Undisputed) lightweight champion](/wiki/List_of_WBA_world_champions#Lightweight "List of WBA world champions")[[a]](#cite_note-a-11) (135 lbs)
* [IBF lightweight champion](/wiki/List_of_IBF_world_champions#Lightweight "List of IBF world champions") (135 lbs)
* [WBO lightweight champion](/wiki/List_of_WBO_world_champions#Lightweight "List of WBO world champions") (135 lbs)

### Minor world titles

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Juan_D%C3%ADaz_(boxer)&action=edit&section=17 "Edit section: Minor world titles")]

* [WBC](/wiki/World_Boxing_Council "World Boxing Council") Youth [lightweight](/wiki/Lightweight "Lightweight") champion (135 lbs)
* [IBO lightweight champion](/wiki/List_of_IBO_world_champions#Lightweight "List of IBO world champions") (135 lbs)

### Regional/International titles

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Juan_D%C3%ADaz_(boxer)&action=edit&section=18 "Edit section: Regional/International titles")]

* [NABO](/wiki/North_American_Boxing_Organization "North American Boxing Organization") [light welterweight](/wiki/Light_welterweight "Light welterweight") champion (140 lbs)

### Honorary titles

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Juan_D%C3%ADaz_(boxer)&action=edit&section=19 "Edit section: Honorary titles")]

* [WBO Super Champion](/wiki/WBO_Super "WBO Super")
